title: 12V → 1.2V Server Point-of-Load
description: A quad-phase synchronous buck delivering 30 A at 1.2 V to a server processor core-voltage rail. Uses interleaved phases to cancel output ripple and distribute heat across four inductors.
design_notes: At 30 A load and 1.2 V output, a single-phase design requires a 7.5 A peak-to-peak inductor ripple current, demanding a very large output capacitor bank to hit 12 mV ripple. Four interleaved phases at 600 kHz each reduce the effective ripple frequency to 2.4 MHz, allowing the per-phase inductance to be lowered and the total capacitance to be dramatically reduced (the ripple cancellation factor K approaches 0 at D = 0.25 with 4 phases). Synchronous rectification is essential at 30 A: a Schottky diode would dissipate ~10 W in the freewheeling phase alone. Use a dedicated multiphase controller IC (e.g. TI TPS40400, Renesas RAA228228) and place all inductors in a row above the output capacitor bank for optimal current sharing.
source: TI SLVA354A — Multiphase Buck Regulator Design; Erickson & Maksimovic §12.4
